title: First K-Nova rezoning hearing on the parcel north of Foxfire
body: The Village of Commercial Point held a public hearing on a K-Nova rezoning request to enable industrial use on the Jahn Farm parcel. Residents raised traffic, property value, wildlife, and rural-character concerns. An attorney delivered more than 200 signatures in opposition.

source.label: First K-Nova rezoning hearing on the parcel north of Foxfire (source)

why_it_matters: Establishes early documented resident opposition.

title: Follow-up K-Nova public hearing on revised plans and landscaping
body: At a follow-up K-Nova public hearing, K-Nova representatives said they had submitted substitute plans, zoning text, and landscaping commitments. Residents discussed mounds, fencing, traffic lights, truck traffic on Durrett Road, warehouse lighting, diesel smell, and continued opposition.

source.label: Follow-up K-Nova public hearing on revised plans and landscaping (source)

why_it_matters: Earliest K-Nova-side commitments on landscaping and buffering.

title: Pickaway County records the Jahn / K-Nova annexation petition
body: Pickaway County Commissioner minutes record an annexation petition filing for approximately 267.59 acres from Scioto Township into Commercial Point. Petitioners listed as Eric Young as Trustee of the James R. Jahn Trust, Alice N. Jahn, and K-Nova LLC. Agent: Craig Moncrief of Plank Law Firm.

source.label: Pickaway County records the Jahn / K-Nova annexation petition (source)

why_it_matters: Start of the official annexation trail.

title: Village adopts statement of services for the Jahn / K-Nova annexation
body: Resolution 17-2023 adopted a statement of services for the proposed 267.59-acre annexation from Scioto Township. The village committed to potable water, zoning, police, refuse, and street services. Resolution stated the village did not then have sanitary sewer capacity available and only estimated, without guaranteeing, capacity within two years.

source.label: Village adopts statement of services for the Jahn / K-Nova annexation (source)

why_it_matters: Sewer-capacity caveat is important for later data center utility analysis.

title: Pickaway County approves the Jahn / K-Nova annexation petition
body: Pickaway County Commissioners approved the annexation petition for approximately 267.59 acres into Commercial Point. County minutes reference parcel L2700010053101 and state that all property owners in the territory signed the petition.

source.label: Pickaway County approves the Jahn / K-Nova annexation petition (source)

why_it_matters: County-level approval before the village annexation ordinance.

title: Data centers added as a permitted use in the Limited Manufacturing District
body: Council amended the zoning code (Chapter 1171.02(c)) to authorize data centers as permissible under the Limited Manufacturing District classification. Declared an emergency.

source.label: Data centers added as a permitted use in the Limited Manufacturing District (source)

why_it_matters: First village action expressly making data centers a permitted use.

title: Sewer code amended for cooling water and industrial process water
body: Ordinance 2023-10 amended Section 1042.05 to allow the Village Administrator to consider requests and issue permits for discharge of cooling water and unpolluted industrial process waters into the sanitary sewer.

source.label: Sewer code amended for cooling water and industrial process water (source)

why_it_matters: Data centers raise water/wastewater questions; this ordinance is part of the utility timeline.

title: K-Nova attorney describes Jahn property as a data center site to Council
body: At Committee of the Whole, Bruce Moore (attorney for K-Nova) said the Jahn property would be rezoned PID like the property to the north and that K-Nova had been receiving inquiries from data centers. Moore also said he might ask Council to waive the third reading and wanted the property rezoned by year-end.

source.label: K-Nova attorney describes Jahn property as a data center site to Council (source)

why_it_matters: Ties Jahn Farm, K-Nova, PID zoning, data centers, and third-reading pressure together.

title: Water, power, and NDA questions raised at council
body: Dustyn Fox asked whether the Commercial Point aquifer could supply an additional five million gallons per day and what South Central would need to supply an additional fifty megawatts. He referenced a person whose company represents a large tech company in Ohio but declined to speak because of an NDA. Bruce Moore stated his intention to ask Council to waive the third reading on the Jahn property but understood they could not.

source.label: Water, power, and NDA questions raised at council (source)

why_it_matters: Water, power, and NDA questions were already in the public record months before Ord 2024-07 passed.

title: Village annexes 267.59 acres from Scioto Township
body: Ordinance 2023-11 approved annexation of approximately 267.59 acres into Commercial Point at the request of Eric Young as Trustee of the James R. Jahn Trust, Alice Jahn, and K-Nova LLC. Craig Moncrief named as petitioner agent.

source.label: Village annexes 267.59 acres from Scioto Township (source)

why_it_matters: Formal village annexation ordinance for the Jahn / K-Nova acreage.

title: Council accepts K-Nova right-of-way deed near SR 104 and Durrett
body: Resolution 38-2023 accepted a limited warranty deed for approximately 4.308 acres from K-Nova LLC for intended right-of-way use on State Route 104 and Durrett Road. The emergency clause stated the real estate closing between K-Nova and its potential buyer needed to be accomplished as soon as possible.

source.label: Council accepts K-Nova right-of-way deed near SR 104 and Durrett (source)

why_it_matters: The phrase 'potential buyer' is a key records-request target. Likely tied to AWS / Amazon Data Services.

title: K-Nova files first 2024 rezoning application for the Jahn parcel
body: K-Nova filed a rezoning application to rezone 266.971 acres from Exceptional Use to Planned Industrial District. Became Ordinance 2024-02, which later died for lack of motion April 1, 2024.

source.label: K-Nova files first 2024 rezoning application for the Jahn parcel (source)

why_it_matters: First 2024 rezoning attempt. Failed before Ord 2024-07 version passed.

title: Public hearing on the first K-Nova rezoning attempt
body: M/I Homes' Josh Barkan said M/I wanted to maintain a residential environment for Foxfire homes and had been talking with K-Nova about mounds, buffering, and landscaping along the southern border. Residents raised concerns about health, environment, noise, light, air, public-vote questions, police coverage, and electrical substations.

source.label: Public hearing on the first K-Nova rezoning attempt (source)

why_it_matters: Foxfire and nearby resident concerns were already in the public record before the May 2024 approval.

title: Sunshine Law concern raised about K-Nova attorney contacting council members
body: At Committee of the Whole, Jay Weaver said Bruce Moore had been contacting council members individually. Mayor Goldhardt said if K-Nova needs to talk to Council they should come to a public meeting and address Council as a whole, and agreed the developer was 'skirting around the Sunshine Law.' Solicitor Mattes warned that emails and personal phone or text communications used for village business can be public records. The same meeting included AWS-property TIF discussion: K-Nova would still collect generated TIF money even if AWS, as a new parcel owner, activated the TIF.

source.label: Sunshine Law concern raised about K-Nova attorney contacting council members (source)

why_it_matters: Strongest source for public-records, Sunshine Law, and individual-contact concerns.

title: Ordinance 2024-02 dies for lack of motion at council
body: On its third reading, Ordinance 2024-02 received no motion to adopt and a separate motion to amend exhibits died for lack of a second, so the rezoning request was denied due to lack of a motion. Public speakers included Ron Jahn, Eric Young, Josh Barkan (M/I), and Bruce Moore (K-Nova). Bruce Moore said the property would be used for data centers only, no warehouses.

source.label: Ordinance 2024-02 dies for lack of motion at council (source)

why_it_matters: Ties Jahn family statements, M/I buffering, K-Nova statements, and the data-center-only claim together.

title: K-Nova files a second 2024 rezoning application for the same 266.971 acres
body: K-Nova filed a new rezoning application for the same 266.971 acres from Exceptional Use to Planned Industrial District. Application became Ordinance 2024-07, adopted on May 20, 2024.

source.label: K-Nova files a second 2024 rezoning application for the same 266.971 acres (source)

why_it_matters: Second 2024 application within 48 hours of the waiver vote.

title: Council amends Chapter 1143.04(e), shortens resubmission rule
body: Council adopted Ordinance 2024-05 amending Chapter 1143.04(e). Decision window changed from 45 days to 120 days; resubmission bar shortened from one year to six months. Mayor explained that waiving readings required 75 percent, which he described as five of six. Laura Wolfe's resignation was effective the same day.

source.label: Council amends Chapter 1143.04(e), shortens resubmission rule (source)

why_it_matters: Council rewrote the same rule that controlled K-Nova's refiling, in the same five-week window in which the rule was waived for one applicant.

title: Council rezones 266.971 acres to Planned Industrial District for data centers
body: Council voted 4-1 to rezone the K-Nova / Jahn Farm parcel from Exceptional Use to Planned Industrial District. Geiger, Nungester, Weaver, Ratliff yes. Crego no. Both a vote on suspension of readings and a separate vote on passage. Standards permit data centers as the only use on the property and allow utilities to be aboveground where absolutely necessary. The same date's public hearing minutes list four members present (Geiger, Weaver, Nungester, Crego), an attendance discrepancy that the special-meeting record needs to resolve.

source.label: Council rezones 266.971 acres to Planned Industrial District for data centers (source)

why_it_matters: The central approval. Two-year ORC 713.121 procedural-challenge ceiling runs to about May 20, 2026.

title: Council approves a major site plan for Scannell Properties on behalf of K-Nova
body: Council approved a major site plan application filed by Scannell Properties #627 LLC on behalf of K-Nova. Same meeting confirmed village wastewater plant capacity at 1.4 million gallons per day with current flows around 400,000 to 500,000 gallons per day.

source.label: Council approves a major site plan for Scannell Properties on behalf of K-Nova (source)

why_it_matters: Confirms K-Nova-Scannell relationship and gives baseline wastewater numbers.

title: Council prohibits gas-fired turbine generation in all zoning districts
body: Ordinance 2026-03 prohibits gas-fired electric generation facilities using turbines in all zoning districts. No zoning certificate, conditional use permit, variance, or other approval may be issued for such facilities.

source.label: Council prohibits gas-fired turbine generation in all zoning districts (source)

why_it_matters: Tied to data-center power-generation concerns; not the same as a moratorium.

title: AWS NDA timeline raised, Mayor Geiger sends non-renewal notice
body: Resident Corey Wasmus stated publicly that the village entered a three-year non-disclosure agreement with Amazon Data Services in July 2023, which would automatically renew in July 2026 unless written notice was provided at least 90 days before renewal. Mayor Nancy Geiger later said she worked with the Village Solicitor to send notice that she would not sign a renewal.

source.label: AWS NDA timeline raised, Mayor Geiger sends non-renewal notice (source)

why_it_matters: First public-record acknowledgment of the AWS NDA terms and the village's non-renewal posture.

title: Council Member Nungester says the Durrett Road site allows data centers by right
body: At the same Feb 23 council meeting, Council Member Eric Nungester stated that based on prior zoning decisions the Durrett Road site allows data center development by right and that Council would have limited ability to control the outcome if the applicant meets existing code requirements.

source.label: Council Member Nungester says the Durrett Road site allows data centers by right (source)

why_it_matters: Tied to the procedural-defect / vested-rights questions.

title: Council Member Miller submits a proposed data center moratorium
body: Council Member Ezekiel Miller submitted a proposed resolution for a moratorium to prevent construction of data centers within the village. Resolution sent to Mayor Geiger and Solicitor Mattes, approved as to form, with minor language and emergency-language changes added. As of last public review, moratorium has not been confirmed adopted.

source.label: Council Member Miller submits a proposed data center moratorium (source)

why_it_matters: Active legislative pushback from current council.
